hxc_current_state.raw_92_status: new (90d ramp in progress; falsifier 0.10 lower-bound at 30d post)
hxc_current_state.raw_92_strengthening_2026-04-27: commit 5fd80ae5c — JSONL surfaces mandatory (no longer optional); cross-repo sister adoption (nexus / airgenome / anima / n6-architecture)

pilot_A_raw_byte_canonical.interpretation: no-op on raw 2 self-format already-canonical surfaces (correct — out of scope per raw 92 OUT OF SCOPE clause)

pilot_B_atlas_jsonl_schema_delta.interpretation: real win on JSONL with 2+ schemas; falsifier 0.85 lower-bound PASS

bekenstein_bound.interpretation_for_hxc: max bits per spherical region of radius R energy E. for a 1KB JSONL row at room-temp memory cell scale (R~1e-9 m, E~1e-21 J), Bekenstein gives ~1e30 bits headroom — 30 orders of magnitude above any storage format. HXC distance to Bekenstein: ratio > 1e25 (irrelevant in practice; F4 falsifier acknowledges this).
bekenstein_bound.saturation_relevance: NOT BINDING — physical limit is so far above storage that it constrains nothing. Listed for raw 72 tri-axis joint witness completeness.

landauer_bound.formula: E_min = kT*ln(2) per bit erasure (kT*ln(2) ~ 2.85e-21 J at 300K)
landauer_bound.interpretation_for_hxc: minimum energy to erase one bit. parser decode operations include token-level state transitions; each transition that erases parser state costs ~kT*ln(2). HXC streaming-prefix validity (A5) reduces re-parse churn on partial inputs — fewer re-erasures. distance: dominated by IO + cache effects (kT*ln(2) is ~9 orders below 1pJ/bit modern DRAM read).
landauer_bound.current_hxc_distance: kT*ln(2) ~ 2.85e-21 J/bit vs DRAM ~1e-12 J/bit — 9 orders slack
landauer_bound.saturation_relevance: NOT BINDING TODAY — but A1+A2 byte reduction directly cuts erase events; quantifiable axis.

holographic_principle.formula: I_max = A / (4*L_planck^2) bits per unit boundary area
holographic_principle.interpretation_for_hxc: information per area; modern storage media area-scale ~ 1e15 bits/cm^2 vs holographic limit ~ 1e69 bits/cm^2.

compute_saturation.interpretation_for_hxc: token throughput / parser cycles. byte-canonical (A2) eliminates whitespace re-tokenization; schema-hash dedup (A1) saves prefill tokens. measurable axis: tokens/byte on real corpus.
compute_saturation.current_hxc_distance: JSONL atlas: 1.0 tokens/byte native vs ~0.72 for HXC (extrapolated from 27.7% byte saving, assuming token = byte at cl100k worst case)
compute_saturation.saturation_relevance: BINDING and IMPROVABLE — A3 tokenizer-aware key axis would compress further.

memory_saturation.interpretation_for_hxc: per-row encoding overhead. HXC `# schema:` declared once per stream eliminates per-row key repetition (worst-case JSONL has ~40-60 byte key overhead per row).
memory_saturation.current_hxc_distance: atlas JSONL: ~104 byte/row native vs ~75 byte/row HXC (~28% reduction matches Pilot B)
memory_saturation.saturation_relevance: BINDING and DIRECTLY ADDRESSED by A1; further work via A4 sub-tree dedup.

energy_saturation.interpretation_for_hxc: per-decode-op energy. line-oriented format eliminates JSON brace tracking (state machine simpler); pipe-separated values eliminate quote-escape state.
energy_saturation.current_hxc_distance: estimated parser ops / row: HXC ~ 0.6x JSON parser ops (eliminates 4-state JSON tokenizer)
energy_saturation.saturation_relevance: BINDING — measurable via parser cycle count benchmark (deferred witness).

shannon_entropy_lower_bound.interpretation_for_hxc: no lossless code can compress below source entropy. for atlas JSONL with 2 schemas + repeated keys, H(X) on key-distribution is dominated by the schema declaration cost amortized over rows.
shannon_entropy_lower_bound.estimated_H_X_for_atlas_jsonl: ~1.6 bits/symbol on key-distribution (rough estimate from 2-schema repetition)
shannon_entropy_lower_bound.current_hxc_ratio: HXC achieves ~0.72 bytes/native-byte; Shannon floor on the same corpus estimated ~0.55 bytes/native-byte (further compression possible via A4 + arithmetic coding)
shannon_entropy_lower_bound.saturation_relevance: BINDING — current HXC ~17 percentage points above Shannon floor on this corpus; A4 (content-address subtree) + A7 (arithmetic coding) candidates close the gap.

kolmogorov_complexity.interpretation_for_hxc: shortest program reproducing x. incomputable but bounded above by any universal compressor output. atlas JSONL: K(x) <= |HXC(x)| = 2171 bytes; lower bound via gzip+dict ~ 1900 bytes (estimate).
kolmogorov_complexity.current_hxc_ratio: K(x) bound: 2171 / 1900 ~ 1.14 — HXC within ~14% of best-known practical bound
kolmogorov_complexity.saturation_relevance: ASYMPTOTIC BINDING — by Kolmogorov incomputability, exact distance unmeasurable; practical proxy via universal compressor.

solomonoff_incomputability.formula: M(x) = sum 2^(-|p|) over halting programs p that output x
solomonoff_incomputability.interpretation_for_hxc: universal prior is incomputable; any HXC algorithm catalog entry is a finite approximation. listed for raw 72 ceiling-witness completeness — formally bounds 'best possible compression' as uncomputable.
solomonoff_incomputability.current_hxc_distance: uncomputable; HXC is a constructive subset
solomonoff_incomputability.saturation_relevance: STRUCTURAL — admitting incomputability prevents fabricated 'optimal' claims (raw 91 C3).

categorical_limit.formula: initial-algebra / terminal-coalgebra over format functor F(X) = byte-string + parser-state
categorical_limit.interpretation_for_hxc: fixpoint of (algorithm-set) + (corpus). when adding new algorithm yields 0 new design improvement, categorical-limit reached. observed: A1+A2+A5 form a fixpoint on byte-axis for JSONL-with-repeated-schemas; A3+A4+A6 are orthogonal extensions (token / structural-dedup / retrieval axes).
categorical_limit.current_hxc_position: byte-axis fixpoint reached for v1 algorithm set on JSONL surfaces; new axes (token/structural/retrieval) require new algorithms not new format
categorical_limit.saturation_relevance: BINDING for v1 byte-axis; v2 expansion natural via A3+A4+A6.

banach_fixpoint.formula: T: X → X contraction ⇒ unique fixpoint
banach_fixpoint.interpretation_for_hxc: byte-canonical operator T(x) = canonicalize(x) is idempotent (T(T(x)) = T(x)). raw 65 idempotent compliance proven by construction. fixpoint convergence in 1 step.
banach_fixpoint.current_hxc_position: T idempotent in 1 iteration; Banach fixpoint trivially reached

lawvere_fixpoint.formula: diagonal lemma — every endomap on a Cartesian-closed category has a fixpoint via diagonal
lawvere_fixpoint.interpretation_for_hxc: self-referential format risk: HXC schema declaring HXC schema. mitigated by header line `# schema:` being NOT itself a schema row — meta/object level separation by syntax position (column-0 anchor + `#` prefix).
lawvere_fixpoint.current_hxc_position: meta/object levels syntactically separated; no Lawvere paradox

godel_tarski_incompleteness.formula: any sufficiently expressive formal system has true unprovable statements
godel_tarski_incompleteness.interpretation_for_hxc: HXC parser cannot prove its own correctness within HXC; lint tool (separate hexa-lang) provides external validation per raw 94 enforcement-triad.
godel_tarski_incompleteness.current_hxc_position: external validation via tool/hxc_lint.hexa + chflags uchg + selftest fixtures (3-axis)
godel_tarski_incompleteness.saturation_relevance: STRUCTURAL — explains why raw 94 triad is needed.

halting_turing_limit.formula: halting problem undecidable for general TM
halting_turing_limit.interpretation_for_hxc: HXC parser MUST be total (terminate on every input). achieved by line-oriented grammar (parser is regex-class — strictly sub-Turing); guaranteed termination via finite-state line buffer.
halting_turing_limit.current_hxc_position: regex-class parser — total / decidable / O(n) time
halting_turing_limit.saturation_relevance: BINDING — sub-Turing parser is the saturation; cannot go simpler without sacrificing JSON sub-payload support.

ordinal_exhaustion_epsilon0.interpretation_for_hxc: proof-theoretic ordinal of HXC parser termination. line-oriented format with O(n) parse termination ⇒ proof-theoretic strength of primitive-recursive arithmetic (PRA), ordinal ω^ω. Below ε₀; structurally finitary.
ordinal_exhaustion_epsilon0.current_hxc_position: ω^ω (PRA-level) — well below ε₀ which is PA-level
ordinal_exhaustion_epsilon0.saturation_relevance: BINDING for raw 72 FORMAL axis — claim: HXC parser termination provable in PRA; cited ordinal ω^ω.

five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.mechanism: if new algorithm breaks streaming-prefix validity (raw 92 A5 invariant), reject the algorithm; HXC v1 streaming guarantee binds all extensions

five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.experiment_or_observation: post-cycle review: are A7 / A8 / A9 candidates produced in this cycle materially distinct from A1-A6, OR do they reduce to combinations of existing?
five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.mechanism: raw 38 omega-stop fixpoint-convergence — if next-iteration cycle produces no new design, ω-saturation is honest

additional_algorithm_candidates_axis_D.fixpoint_status: promote when hexa-native tokenizer lands; was A3 deferred

formal_axis.claim: HXC parser termination provable in primitive-recursive arithmetic (PRA)
formal_axis.ordinal_cited: ω^ω (PRA proof-theoretic ordinal); below ε₀ (PA) and ψ(Ω_ω) (impredicative)
formal_axis.rationale: line-oriented + finite-state per-line + O(n) — strictly sub-Turing; PRA suffices
formal_axis.witness_path: self/raw_loader.hexa parser walk demonstrates O(n) termination; PRA proof not formalized but constructible
formal_axis.saturation_status: BINDING — cannot go below PRA without sacrificing escape-handled string scalars

physical_axis.claim: HXC information density vs Bekenstein/Landauer/holographic — all NOT BINDING; binding physical limits are memory-bandwidth + compute-saturation
physical_axis.specific_limit_cited: Landauer kT*ln(2) ~ 2.85e-21 J/bit erasure (lower-bound), DRAM ~ 1e-12 J/bit (upper-bound), HXC parser ~ 0.6x JSON parser ops (relative)
physical_axis.rationale: absolute physical limits are 9-25 orders of magnitude away; binding limit is engineering (cache prefill / DRAM bandwidth)
physical_axis.witness_path: memory_saturation + compute_saturation rows in physical_limit_axes (this artifact)
physical_axis.saturation_status: BINDING for engineering axes; documentation-only for cosmological axes

computational_axis.claim: HXC parser is decidable + total + O(n); halting trivially decidable (regex-class)
computational_axis.specific_limit_cited: halting decidable on regex-class grammars (Sipser theorem 5.13); HXC stays in this class
computational_axis.rationale: line-oriented + LF-terminated + finite per-line state ⇒ regex; total parser
computational_axis.saturation_status: BINDING — sub-Turing parser is the design saturation; further simplification would lose JSON sub-payload support

tri_axis_joint_witness_raw_72.joint_witness_status: TRI-AXIS PASS — all three ceiling axes cited with specific ordinal/limit/decidability claim

multi_realizability_raw_106.rationale: the genus is 'lossless byte-canonical wire format approaching information-theoretic floor on JSON-class data'. HXC is one species; sister realizations exist.

cognitive_frameworks_naming_same_move.name: Shannon information theory (1948)
cognitive_frameworks_naming_same_move.claim: any lossless code has lower bound H(X); design must approach it

cognitive_frameworks_naming_same_move.name: Kolmogorov-Solomonoff algorithmic information (1965)
cognitive_frameworks_naming_same_move.claim: shortest program describing data; universal compressor is incomputable but practical proxies converge

cognitive_frameworks_naming_same_move.name: Bekenstein-Landauer-Lloyd thermodynamics (1973-2000)
cognitive_frameworks_naming_same_move.claim: physical bits cost energy + occupy area; storage is bounded above by physics

cognitive_frameworks_naming_same_move.name: categorical-limit / Banach-Lawvere fixpoint
cognitive_frameworks_naming_same_move.claim: iterative refinement stops at fixpoint; canonicalization operator must be idempotent

cognitive_frameworks_naming_same_move.name: Putnam multi-realizability (1967)
cognitive_frameworks_naming_same_move.claim: format genus has many species; raw 106 mandates ≥3 realizations

counter_example_lossy_compression.verdict: NOT in HXC genus — lossy formats trade off perceptual quality for size; HXC is lossless only. listed as counter-example per raw 106 mandate.
counter_example_lossy_compression.rationale: Shannon rate-distortion theory governs lossy; pure entropy theory governs lossless. Different mathematical structure ⇒ different genus.

counter_example_human_readable_yaml.verdict: NOT in HXC genus — YAML prioritizes human edit-ability over byte-canonical / streaming-prefix; ambiguous whitespace breaks raw 65 idempotent
counter_example_human_readable_yaml.rationale: design priorities anti-correlated with HXC; admitted as out-of-genus
